title: avoira scandens
intro: Avoira scandens is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, also known as the 'Cascabela thevetia' or 'Yellow Oleander', belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anthraquinone and cardenolide content. The health benefits of Avoira scandens include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain. Herbal preparations of Avoira scandens can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, although caution should be exercised due to the plant's toxic nature.
